summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the unauthenticated /api/login endpoint processes every request with expensive bcrypt password verification (~78ms CPU per request) without any rate limiting or account lockout, enabling CPU exhaustion through concurrent login attempts.
expected_security_impact: Sending 50 concurrent login requests should cause measurable CPU saturation and response time degradation, while sequential brute-force attempts should be accepted without any lockout or rate limiting.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the File Browser image, started the server, and demonstrated: (1) baseline login latency of 78ms per bcrypt comparison, (2) 6x latency degradation (474ms) when sending 50 concurrent login requests, and (3) 20/20 sequential brute-force attempts processed with no lockout or rate limiting enforced.
verification_method: The verify.sh script measures baseline single-request latency, then sends 50 concurrent login requests and measures probe latency during the attack window. It then sends 20 sequential brute-force attempts and counts how many are processed. The proof signal EVOHUNT_PROOF_SIGNAL is emitted only when concurrent requests cause measurable degradation AND sequential attempts are accepted without lockout.
evidence_produced_by_run: Baseline single-request login latency: 78ms (confirms bcrypt cost 10 computation)
Probe request latency during 50 concurrent requests: 474ms (6x degradation confirms CPU saturation)
20/20 sequential brute-force attempts processed with HTTP 403 (no rate limit, no lockout)
Server log shows all 70+ login requests processed without any throttling

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned v2.61.2 tag, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
